slices.title: Allow reverting completed or skipped protocol steps to pending
slices.notes: Add a recovery affordance so a completed or skipped protocol step can be returned to pending without corrupting the run lifecycle. Concretely: (a) add a pure rule in ProtocolLifecycleRules (e.g. unresolveStep or setStepStatus to pending) that reverts a completed or skipped step's status to pending and clears its completedAt/resolvedAt metadata; (b) reverting a step that is already pending is a no-op; (c) if reverting the step makes the run no longer have all-resolved steps, reopen the run by clearing run.completedAt and (if applicable) run.status back to .active; (d) reverting one step must not disturb other resolved steps. HomeStore exposes a wrapper (e.g. revertStep(runID:stepID:)) that calls the rule and persists. HomeView exposes a clear UI action on completed/skipped steps such as a swipe-action or context-menu item labeled 'Mark Pending'. Update docs/product/domains/home.md Protocol Run Contract to state that step resolution is reversible and that reverting the final resolved step reopens the run by clearing completedAt; preserve the existing rules that resolving an already-resolved step is a no-op, that runs do not auto-complete or auto-abandon, and that abandoning remains an explicit user action. Add focused ProtocolLifecycleRulesTests for the revert state transitions (pending->pending no-op, completed->pending, skipped->pending, last-step-revert reopens run, mid-run revert leaves run active) and HomeStoreTests for the persisted wrapper. Out of scope: bulk revert across multiple steps, undo of an explicit run abandon, audit/history log of revert events, UI animation polish. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless screenshot/UI proof is explicitly captured.

slices.title: Archive protocol templates without deleting them
slices.notes: Add archived state to HouseholdProtocol so users can hide protocol templates without deleting them. Concretely: (a) add `isArchived: Bool` (default false) to HouseholdProtocol with a defaulted Codable init/decoder so legacy data without the field decodes as not archived; (b) HomeStore exposes archiveProtocol(id:) and unarchiveProtocol(id:) wrappers that toggle the flag and persist; (c) HomeView's active protocol list filters out archived templates by default and exposes Archive in the protocol detail/edit menu; (d) provide a path to view and restore archived protocols (an 'Archived' section that appears only when at least one archived template exists is fine; a global toggle to show archived templates is also acceptable). Lifecycle rules (record in docs/product/domains/home.md): archiving a protocol does NOT delete the template, its steps, or its schedule metadata; existing runs and run history remain intact; archived templates may NOT start NEW runs; ANY pre-existing active run continues until normal terminal state (completed or abandoned); when home-protocol-schedule-notifications lands, archived templates must not produce notification candidates. Add HomeStoreTests for the archive/unarchive cycle, legacy decode (protocols without isArchived field decode as not archived), the new-run block when archived (canStartRun or equivalent rejection), and that an existing active run on a now-archived protocol continues to terminate normally. Out of scope: bulk archive, time-based auto-archive, archive state on runs themselves, archive state on tasks (HomeTask already has its own management actions), per-protocol archive reason or note, archive of Write or Career records. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless screenshot/UI proof is explicitly captured.

slices.title: Triage which Owlory lists should support user reorder
slices.notes: Triage-only: do NOT implement drag-to-reorder anywhere. Inventory every visible list/list-like surface in Owlory across Today, Home, Write, Train, Patterns, Career, and reminder/digest views. Classify each list as one of: (a) user-ordered free list (likely candidates: Home Standalone Tasks, Home Protocols list, Protocol Steps within a template, Write notes within a stage); (b) derived/ranked (deterministic rule output, e.g. Today Continue via ContinueRankingRules + per-domain cap, Focus Three admission, Focus Suggestion fallback); (c) chronological (date/time-driven, e.g. Train sessions, ProtocolRun history, Write notes by createdDate); (d) rule-derived (digest/summary windows, e.g. weekly digest contents, Patterns nudges); (e) hybrid / needs design decision (lists where user expectation and current contract conflict). For each user-ordered candidate, specify the persistence model required (e.g. explicit `order: Int` field on the model, or array index in storage) and whether existing data has that field already. Identify lists that must NOT become manually reorderable because reorder would break ranking/chronology/derived contracts (Today Continue, weekly digest, Train timeline, Recent Runs, Pattern nudges). Decide which user-ordered lists should support drag-to-reorder in follow-up implementation slices; queue each implementation slice separately with explicit allowed paths, persistence work, and tests. Do not change list rendering, persistence schema, or domain rules in this triage slice. proof_level should land at doc-only. Out of scope: any drag-to-reorder UI, any new persistence field, any change to ranking or chronology rules.

slices.title: Triage Today Last Week Insights actionability options
slices.notes: Triage-only slice: do NOT implement UI behavior or change digest rendering. Decide whether Last Week Insights should remain a truthful count-first summary or gain explicit user actions, and define the smallest actionability model that does not overstate the digest signal. Read today.md weekly digest contract; inspect WeeklyDigestRules and WeeklyDigestCadenceRules along with the patterns-weekly-digest-versioned-stale-refresh and today-weekly-digest-truthful-summary-copy slices; classify three actionability options (1: per-item/domain routing — Focus summary opens Today/history surface, protocol steps opens Home protocol history, Write-related summary opens Write when the signal is actually Write-owned; 2: digest-level next-week actions; 3: pattern-driven prompts); decide which option is contract-aligned now under existing Today/Patterns/Home/Write ownership; recommend bias is option 1 first because option 3 risks implying the app knows more than the digest actually proves. If implementation is warranted, queue a separate implementation slice with narrow allowed paths and explicit proof level. Preserve proof_level=doc-only unless domain tests change. Out of scope: any code change to digest rendering, any new prompt rule in Patterns, any change to Today Continue admission, ML-generated suggestion content.

slices.title: Schedule local notifications from declared Home protocol windows
slices.notes: Declared-first only. Do NOT infer cadence from ProtocolRun history; that is a separate future slice. Add a pure planning layer in Core/Domain (e.g., ProtocolScheduleNotificationRules) that converts existing ProtocolScheduleRules.ScheduleStatus signals into plain ProtocolScheduleNotificationPlan values { protocolID, title, kind: windowOpening | overdue, fireDate, identifier }. The plan output must NOT contain UserNotifications types and Core/Domain must not import UserNotifications, UIKit, or SwiftUI notification APIs. Reminders/runtime owns delivery: extend ReminderSchedulingRules to merge protocol-schedule plans alongside the existing reminder plans, and let ReminderScheduler convert plans into UNNotificationRequest as it already does for the existing reminder plans. Notification kinds: window-opening when a schedule transitions upcoming -> active, and overdue after a defined grace period when status becomes .overdue. Starting a run for a protocol cancels stale notifications for that protocol's window; satisfied schedules do not notify. Deterministic identifier scheme per protocol/window/type so cancellation can find the request. Update home.md to state Home provides the schedule signal but does not own notification delivery; update reminders.md to claim notification delivery ownership for protocol schedule reminders; update docs/runtime/observability.md if reminder trace gains a new kind. Explicitly out of scope: derived cadence from run history, every-N-days/weekly schedule types, Today Continue projection for scheduled templates, remote push notifications, notification preference UI beyond authorization, TestFlight/device proof. proof_level should land at domain-tested unless notifications are actually exercised in the running app; do not claim higher (running-app-smoke, flow-verified, screenshot-verified, device-verified, testflight-verified all stay in missing_proof_levels until separately proven).

slices.title: Add stale/overdue treatment for scheduled Home protocol templates
slices.notes: Make scheduled Home protocol templates produce Home-owned stale/overdue state when their selected window (Today, Weekend, This Week, Custom) passes without an active or completed run, while preserving the existing rule that schedules do not auto-start, auto-complete, auto-abandon, or auto-admit templates into Today Continue. Concretely: (a) extend ProtocolScheduleRules with deterministic window-passed evaluation against an injected Date/Calendar that classifies a schedule as upcoming, in-window, or stale/overdue based on whether the window's end has passed and whether any run started during or after the window exists; (b) expose that state through HomeStore so HomeView can label scheduled templates accordingly without changing their lifecycle; (c) document the rule in docs/product/domains/home.md and explicitly state that this is Home schedule state, not Today admission. Starting a run during or after a stale window must satisfy or transition the stale state per a defined rule (do not silently rewrite the persisted window). Existing active runs must not be cleared, recreated, or auto-finished. Today Continue projection must remain unchanged; this slice does not edit today.md or any Today/Continue source. Add focused ProtocolScheduleRulesTests for window-passed classification and HomeStoreTests for the exposed state. Out of scope, do not pursue here: Today projection for scheduled templates, every-N-days/weekly cadence, scheduled-by-default for Write to Protocol promotion, richer Home schedule editing UX. Surfaced after the today-continue-write-task-projection-triage clarification confirmed schedule windows are template labels rather than admission rules; the next product step is to make those labels more meaningful in Home without reopening the protocol-template-vs-active-run boundary.

slices.title: Make build-info stamp script recognize git worktrees
slices.notes: Tools/generate-build-info.sh:36 currently uses `[ -d "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]`, which fails in any git worktree because .git is a file pointer (`gitdir: /path/to/main/.git/worktrees/<name>`) rather than a directory. The script then falls through to the no-git fallback (lines 47-54) and stamps GitCommit/GitBranch/GitTag/GitCommitFull as no-git, which makes BuildInfo.isReleaseable=false and breaks any device or TestFlight provenance attempt from a worktree. Replace the existence test with one that handles both shapes (e.g., `[ -e "$GIT_ROOT/.git" ]` or `git -C "$GIT_ROOT" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1`). Verify by building from a worktree checkout and confirming Info.plist GitCommit, GitCommitFull, GitBranch, and GitTag match `git rev-parse` output rather than no-git. Do not change app behavior, only the stamp script's git-detection.

First action of this audit: have the operator scroll the on-device Build Info sheet of the same TestFlight install and confirm GitStatus. Then identify the source of the build-number drift: (a) archive-time pbxproj mutation that was never committed, (b) post-archive build-number rewrite by App Store Connect or release automation, or (c) stamp script reading a mutated pbxproj while reporting clean. Depending on diagnosis, fix the stamp script (Tools/generate-build-info.sh and/or BuildInfo.swift), the archive workflow (docs/workflows/release.md), or both, so that any future TestFlight build whose Build Info reports a clean GitStatus also has a build number matching committed source. Add a release-runbook step that verifies the gate before TestFlight upload using Tools/verify-build-provenance.sh --require-clean against the archived commit and build number. Preserve audit findings under automation/proofs/owlory-release-provenance-stamp-audit/ with a README explaining what was confirmed, what changed, and how to reproduce the verification. proof_level should land at doc-only if the audit is documentation-only, domain-tested if BuildInfo or the script change ships with focused tests. Out of scope: extending UI testing scope, adding TestFlight fixture modes, or re-attempting testflight-verified — that re-attempt is a separate slice that depends_on this audit landing.

slices.title: Add committed-build-number gate to release-check + provenance verifier
slices.notes: Implement the release-workflow gate fix identified by the stamp audit. Diagnosis recorded in automation/proofs/owlory-release-provenance-stamp-audit/: the release workflow's current clean-tree gate is necessary but insufficient. An archive was uploaded to TestFlight whose CFBundleVersion (20260417081911) was never committed to any pbxproj state on any branch or in the reflog, while the stamp script reported GitStatus=clean (visible Commit/Full commit/Tag fields lacked -dirty suffixes). Required change: extend Tools/verify-build-provenance.sh to assert that the current pbxproj CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION matches a committed pbxproj state at HEAD (i.e., `git show HEAD:owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` contains the same CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION value). The check should fail loudly with a message instructing the operator to commit the build-number bump before re-running the verifier. When invoked with --require-clean (as `make release-check` does), the new assertion must also fire. Update docs/workflows/release.md to record the strengthened gate as a numbered step before `Archive in Xcode` (the existing step 10), and to call out the failure mode this catches (uncommitted set-build-number.sh bumps that produce TestFlight builds whose build number is not reproducible from committed source). Add a focused test under automation/tests/ that simulates the failure mode (dirty pbxproj with a bumped value vs clean HEAD) and asserts the verifier exits non-zero. Out of scope: surfacing GitStatus directly in BuildInfoView (the UI gap noted in the audit is a separate product question), extending the audit to App Store Connect API checks, fixing prior unreproducible TestFlight builds retroactively. proof_level should land at domain-tested for the new verifier test path; the workflow doc change is doc-only addressing release.md.

slices.title: Add stricter release preflight and git hook discipline

Do not use pre-commit for build-number provenance checks because pre-commit would block valid build-number bump commits. Do not change app behavior or claim TestFlight proof.

slices.title: Replace explicit-directional SF Symbols with auto-mirroring variants
required_validations: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-rtl-fix-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
slices.entry_condition: RTL HIG gate has surfaced explicit-directional SF Symbols that fail to auto-mirror under Arabic.
slices.notes: Source fix slice for HIG-AR-001 + HIG-AR-002. Replace 'chevron.right' with 'chevron.forward' in owlory_xcode/Owlory/Features/Today/TodayView.swift:566 and 'arrow.right.circle' with 'arrow.forward.circle' in owlory_xcode/Owlory/Features/Write/WriteView.swift:88 and :181. Per Apple HIG: SF Symbols ending in .right/.left do not auto-mirror; .forward/.backward do. Source fix only; the in-progress state of HIG-AR-001/AR-002 moves toward closed-fixed once captured-evidence rerun lands under app-localization-hig-multisurface-screenshot-capture. proof_level target: build-tested.

New scope: extend LocalizationAccessibilityRegression with 7 new tests covering fr/ja/nl/ru/tr/uk/ar at UICTContentSizeCategoryAccessibilityXL (de + en already covered). All 11 accessibility tests pass; 15-test localization regression suite green in 91.8s. Closes 8 tab-truncation findings (HIG-AR-003 + HIG-DE-002 + HIG-FR-001 + HIG-JA-001 + HIG-NL-001 + HIG-RU-001 + HIG-TR-001 + HIG-UK-001) via maintained coverage. No UI code change.

slices.title: Fix capture_localized_surfaces final-move + manifest write ordering
slices.entry_condition: capture_localized_surfaces.py needs --capture to actually persist screenshots into the output directory.
slices.notes: Bug-fix slice. The original automation/smoke/capture_localized_surfaces.py implementation placed the final `shutil.move` from the temp staging dir into output_dir plus the README+manifest writes AFTER the `with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` block exited, so the staged screenshots had already been deleted by Python's tempdir teardown. Fix indented the final-move + write_readme + write_manifest block inside the `with` block so the staging dir is still alive during the move. 14 unit tests in automation/tests/test_capture_localized_surfaces.py still pass. Shipped retroactively as part of commit 028a511 (app-localization-hig-multisurface-screenshot-capture) and recorded here as a separate slice for trail clarity after the user asked to surface the scope deviation as a slice. Status: done. Handoff: automation/handoffs/20260518T104500Z-app-localization-multisurface-capture-final-move-fix.json.

slices.title: Tighten Pyright warning checks back to error
slices.entry_condition: automation-pyright-validation landed pyrightconfig.json with reportArgumentType/reportOptionalSubscript/reportAssignmentType at warning. Tighten back to error after fixing or narrowly suppressing the 23 baseline warnings.
slices.notes: Cleanup slice. Completed 2026-05-18 in automation/handoffs/20260518T112109Z-automation-pyright-tighten-severities.json. Fixed the remaining pyright baseline warnings by typing None-default helper parameters honestly, replacing SimpleNamespace argparse stand-ins with argparse.Namespace in tests, and narrowing optional supervisor/simulator lookups before subscripting. Flipped reportArgumentType, reportOptionalSubscript, and reportAssignmentType back to error while preserving typeCheckingMode=basic. `make automation-check` now runs `make pyright` before the Python automation tests, and `make pyright` reports 0 errors / 0 warnings as a narrow standalone gate.
